title: campanula rotundifolia subsp. langsdorffiana
intro: Campanula rotundifolia subsp. langsdorffiana, also known as Langsdorff's bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to have potential health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. Additionally, Langsdorff's bellflower has been traditionally used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and other nervous system disorders due to its sedative properties.
